framework.name: Three-Dimensional Agent Evolution Framework (What/When/How)
framework.description: Systematic taxonomy for understanding self-evolving agents across three orthogonal dimensions: WHAT evolves (model/memory/tools/architecture), WHEN evolution occurs (intra-test-time vs inter-test-time), and HOW evolution is implemented (reward-based, feedback-based, single vs multi-agent). This provides a structured way to analyze and design adaptive agent systems.
framework.raw_quote: We organize our analysis around three foundational questions — what, when, and how to evolve — and provide a structured framework for understanding each.
